class Solution {
public:
    string categorizeBox(int length, int width, int height, int mass) {
        long long vol = (long long)length * (long long)width * (long long)height;
        bool is_bulky = (length >= 10000 || width >= 10000 || height >= 10000) || (vol >= 1000000000LL);
        bool is_heavy = (mass >= 100);
        if (is_bulky && is_heavy) {
            return "Both";
        } else if (is_bulky) {
            return "Bulky";
        } else if (is_heavy) {
            return "Heavy";
        } else {
            return "Neither";
        }
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string categorizeBox(int length, int width, int height, int mass) {
        long long vol = (long long)length * (long long)width * (long long)height;
        bool is_bulky = (length >= 10000 || width >= 10000 || height >= 10000) || (vol >= 1000000000LL);
        bool is_heavy = (mass >= 100);
        if (is_bulky && is_heavy) {
            return "Both";
        } else if (is_bulky) {
            return "Bulky";
        } else if (is_heavy) {
            return "Heavy";
        } else {
            return "Neither";
        }
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution checks if the box is bulky by evaluating dimensions and volume, then checks if it's heavy based on mass. It uses direct comparisons and arithmetic operations, ensuring optimal performance with constant time and space complexity. The approach is straightforward and avoids unnecessary computations.
